violations.text: Raw animal foods (pork) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (garlic) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the hoods have an accumulation of grease and soil residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Raw animal foods (pork) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (tofu) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC changed sanitizer at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Noodles were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. hoods 2. prep units 3. stove 4. fryers 5. warewashing shelving New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The bar ice well is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Food was observed held at 45-46 degrees Fahrenheit in the low boy unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food was transferred from the walk-in cooler to the low boy unit about an hour prior to inspection. Food was relocated to the walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Low boy unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Low boy unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Wood is used in the construction of shelves in the dry storage area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the cold-hold units, shelves in the kitchen, and shelves in the dry storage area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Cold-hold units and all shelves must be cleaned. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The sink used for food preparation and ice well are lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

Onions were observed stored in an area susceptible to splash (under sink). Food must be stored in a location that prevents contamination from the premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

Single-service articles (disposable lids and containers) were observed stored on the floor at the bar. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following items have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1) ice machine shield 2) cold-holding equipment 3) shelf under prep table New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

The following items have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. 1) floor in the dry storage area and bar 2) walls behind the 3-bay sink and dishwasher 3) the grill hood Per person in charge, the hood is scheduled to be cleaned next week. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
